title: CLAUDE.md
text: mdgm (Mixture Directed Graphical Models) R package C++20 core library. implements graph data structures sampling algorithms probabilistic graphical models. C++ layer handles computation; R layer provides user-facing APIs via cpp11 bindings R6 classes. paper available https://arxiv.org/html/2406.15700v4.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Graph Storage (graph_storage.hpp/cpp) — Two sparse formats: GraphCOO (coordinate/edge list) GraphCSR (compressed sparse row). COO used construction, CSR efficient queries. Conversion transposition supported. NaturalUndirectedGraph (natural_undirected_graph.hpp/cpp) — Undirected graph built GraphCSR. Key algorithms: SampleSpanningTree() (Wilson’s, Aldous-Broder, FastForward methods) SampleAcyclicOrientation(). GenerateRegularGraph() creates 2D grid graphs. DirectedAcyclicGraph (directed_acyclic_graph.hpp/cpp) — DAG forward (CSR) reverse (transposed) representations parent/child queries. RNG (rng.hpp) — Wrapper around std::mt19937_64 providing uniform, normal, discrete, permutation sampling.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: cpp11 bindings (src/R_natural_undirected_graph.cpp, src/R_utils.cpp) — C++ objects wrapped R external pointers. Functions annotated [[cpp11::register]] auto-registered. R6 class (R/natural_undirected_graph.R) — NaturalUndirectedGraph R6 class wrapping C++ object. User-facing constructors: nug_from_edge_list(), nug_from_adj_list(), nug_from_adj_mat(). Indexing convention: R uses 1-indexed vectors; conversion 0-indexed C++ happens R binding layer (R_natural_undirected_graph.cpp).

title: Emission Models
text: hierarchical model, spatial field zz latent observations yy generated emission distribution. MDGM MRF spatial models support three emission families: emission families enforce identifiability constraint location parameters: p0<p1<⋯p_0 < p_1 < \cdots (Bernoulli), μ0<μ1<⋯\mu_0 < \mu_1 < \cdots (Gaussian), λ0<λ1<⋯\lambda_0 < \lambda_1 < \cdots (Poisson). achieved via truncated conjugate posterior sampling.

title: Emission Models
text: examples use simple region-growing algorithm generate irregular spatial clusters grid graph. Starting random seed vertices, regions expand outward one cell time, producing blocky asymmetric patterns:
code: grow_regions <- function(nug, n_colors, seed = NULL) {   if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)   n <- nug$nvertices()   z <- rep(NA_integer_, n)    # Plant one seed per color   seeds <- sample(n, n_colors)   for (k in seq_along(seeds)) z[seeds[k]] <- k - 1L    # Grow: repeatedly claim a random unclaimed neighbor   repeat {     boundary <- which(!is.na(z))     candidates <- integer(0)     for (v in boundary) {       nbrs <- nug$neighbors(v)       candidates <- c(candidates, nbrs[is.na(z[nbrs])])     }     candidates <- unique(candidates)     if (length(candidates) == 0) break      pick <- sample(candidates, 1)     claimed <- nug$neighbors(pick)     claimed <- claimed[!is.na(z[claimed])]     z[pick] <- z[sample(claimed, 1)]   }   z } nug <- nug_from_grid(8, 8, seed = 42L) n <- nug$nvertices()  z_true <- grow_regions(nug, n_colors = 2, seed = 28)  grid_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:8, 8), y = rep(8:1, each = 8),   z = factor(z_true) ) ggplot(grid_df, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "#440154", "1" = "#fde725")) +   coord_equal() + theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Simulated spatial field (region growing)", fill = "z")

text: Bernoulli emission models binary observations. vertex ii can multiple replicate observations yi1,…,yimiy_{i1}, \ldots, y_{im_i}, drawn independently Bernoulli(pzi)\text{Bernoulli}(p_{z_i}). Multiple replicates per vertex needed identifiability Bernoulli case.

what: Example: binary indicators on a grid
title: Emission Models
text: Fit model:
code: set.seed(1) p_true <- c(0.3, 0.7)  # 5 replicate observations per vertex y_bern <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {   rbinom(5, 1, p_true[z_true[i] + 1]) })  # Visualize average response per vertex obs_df_b <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:8, 8), y = rep(8:1, each = 8),   value = vapply(y_bern, mean, numeric(1)) ) ggplot(obs_df_b, aes(x, y, fill = value)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "#440154", high = "#fde725") +   coord_equal() + theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Average observed response (5 replicates)", fill = expression(bar(y))) model_b <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree"),                      emission = "bernoulli")  result_b <- mcmc(model_b, y = y_bern,                  z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5,                  theta_init = c(0.3, 0.7),                  n_iter = 5000L,                  psi_tune = 1.0,                  store_z = TRUE,                  seed = 42L,                  nug = nug)  result_b$summary(burnin = 1000L) #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 64, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 5000 (burnin: 1000) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.390 #>   Psi posterior mean: 1.5658 (sd: 0.6204) #>   Emission type: bernoulli #>   p_1 posterior mean: 0.3097 (sd: 0.0550) #>   p_2 posterior mean: 0.7461 (sd: 0.0425) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 0.9998, ESS: 204 #>     p_1 — R-hat: 0.9998, ESS: 640 #>     p_2 — R-hat: 1.0022, ESS: 599

title: Emission Models
code: psi_df_b <- data.frame(iteration = seq_along(result_b$psi()), psi = result_b$psi()) ggplot(psi_df_b, aes(iteration, psi)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.3) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Bernoulli: psi trace", x = "Iteration", y = expression(psi)) ep_b <- result_b$emission_params() n_iter <- ncol(ep_b$p)  p_df <- data.frame(   iteration = rep(seq_len(n_iter), 2),   value = c(ep_b$p[1, ], ep_b$p[2, ]),   parameter = rep(c("p_1", "p_2"), each = n_iter) )  ggplot(p_df, aes(iteration, value, color = parameter)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.3) +   geom_hline(yintercept = p_true, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Bernoulli emission: p trace",        x = "Iteration", y = "Value", color = NULL)

title: Emission Models
text: Gaussian emission models continuous observations. observation drawn 𝒩(μzi,σzi2)\mathcal{N}(\mu_{z_i}, \sigma_{z_i}^2). single observation per vertex sufficient identifiability. priors μk\mu_k σk2\sigma_k^2 independent: μk∼𝒩(μ0,σ02),σk2∼InverseGamma(α0,β0)\mu_k \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_0, \sigma^2_0), \qquad \sigma_k^2 \sim \text{InverseGamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0) σ02\sigma^2_0 prior variance mean.

title: Emission Models
text: use overlapping emission distributions — two groups differ mean share similar spread, making classification rely emission signal spatial structure:  Fit Gaussian emission:
code: set.seed(2) mu_true <- c(5, 12) sigma2_true <- c(9, 9)  # Single observation per vertex y_gauss <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {   rnorm(1, mu_true[z_true[i] + 1], sqrt(sigma2_true[z_true[i] + 1])) })  # Visualize the observations obs_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:8, 8), y = rep(8:1, each = 8),   value = vapply(y_gauss, `[`, numeric(1), 1) ) ggplot(obs_df, aes(x, y, fill = value)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#440154", mid = "#21918c", high = "#fde725",                        midpoint = mean(obs_df$value)) +   coord_equal() + theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Observed temperatures (single obs per vertex)", fill = "y") model_g <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree"),                      emission = "gaussian")  # theta_init: c(mu_1, mu_2, sigma2_1, sigma2_2) result_g <- mcmc(model_g, y = y_gauss,                  z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5,                  theta_init = c(4, 14, 9, 9),                  n_iter = 5000L,                  psi_tune = 1.0,                  store_z = TRUE,                  seed = 42L,                  nug = nug)  result_g$summary(burnin = 1000L) #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 64, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 5000 (burnin: 1000) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.379 #>   Psi posterior mean: 1.6287 (sd: 0.7845) #>   Emission type: gaussian #>   mu_1 posterior mean: 5.5150 (sd: 0.9295) #>   mu_2 posterior mean: 12.8036 (sd: 1.0608) #>   sigma2_1 posterior mean: 11.7869 (sd: 4.7116) #>   sigma2_2 posterior mean: 12.8191 (sd: 5.9121) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 1.0025, ESS: 102 #>     mu_1 — R-hat: 1.0008, ESS: 255 #>     mu_2 — R-hat: 1.0012, ESS: 283 #>     sigma2_1 — R-hat: 1.0010, ESS: 292 #>     sigma2_2 — R-hat: 1.0001, ESS: 359

title: Emission Models
code: psi_df_g <- data.frame(iteration = seq_along(result_g$psi()), psi = result_g$psi()) ggplot(psi_df_g, aes(iteration, psi)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.3) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Gaussian: psi trace", x = "Iteration", y = expression(psi)) ep_g <- result_g$emission_params() n_iter_g <- ncol(ep_g$mu)  mu_df <- data.frame(   iteration = rep(seq_len(n_iter_g), 2),   value = c(ep_g$mu[1, ], ep_g$mu[2, ]),   parameter = rep(c("mu_1", "mu_2"), each = n_iter_g) )  ggplot(mu_df, aes(iteration, value, color = parameter)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.3) +   geom_hline(yintercept = mu_true, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Gaussian emission: mu trace",        x = "Iteration", y = "Value", color = NULL) sigma2_df <- data.frame(   iteration = rep(seq_len(n_iter_g), 2),   value = c(ep_g$sigma2[1, ], ep_g$sigma2[2, ]),   parameter = rep(c("sigma2_1", "sigma2_2"), each = n_iter_g) )  ggplot(sigma2_df, aes(iteration, value, color = parameter)) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.3) +   geom_hline(yintercept = sigma2_true, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Gaussian emission: sigma2 trace",        x = "Iteration", y = "Value", color = NULL)

title: Emission Models
text: Poisson emission models count data. observation drawn Poisson(λzi)\text{Poisson}(\lambda_{z_i}). Gaussian case, single observation per vertex sufficient identifiability. prior Gamma distribution: λk∼Gamma(α0,β0)\lambda_k \sim \text{Gamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0) rate parameterization (mean =α0/β0= \alpha_0 / \beta_0).

title: Emission Models
text: use overlapping rates spatial structure contributes meaningfully classification:  Fit Poisson emission:
code: set.seed(3) lambda_true <- c(4, 10)  # Single count per vertex y_pois <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) {   rpois(1, lambda_true[z_true[i] + 1]) })  obs_df_p <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:8, 8), y = rep(8:1, each = 8),   value = vapply(y_pois, `[`, numeric(1), 1) ) ggplot(obs_df_p, aes(x, y, fill = value)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "#440154", high = "#fde725") +   coord_equal() + theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "Observed counts (single obs per vertex)", fill = "y") model_p <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree"),                      emission = "poisson")  result_p <- mcmc(model_p, y = y_pois,                  z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5,                  theta_init = c(3, 8),                  n_iter = 5000L,                  psi_tune = 1.0,                  store_z = TRUE,                  seed = 42L,                  nug = nug)  result_p$summary(burnin = 1000L) #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 64, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 5000 (burnin: 1000) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.412 #>   Psi posterior mean: 1.8797 (sd: 0.7296) #>   Emission type: poisson #>   lambda_1 posterior mean: 4.0756 (sd: 0.5852) #>   lambda_2 posterior mean: 9.0175 (sd: 0.7055) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 1.0000, ESS: 201 #>     lambda_1 — R-hat: 1.0054, ESS: 246 #>     lambda_2 — R-hat: 1.0080, ESS: 390

title: Emission Models
text: three emission types, posterior mode latent field recover true spatial pattern:
code: burnin <- 1000L  recover_field <- function(result, burnin) {   z_post <- result$z()   z_burn <- z_post[, (burnin + 1):ncol(z_post)]   apply(z_burn, 1, function(row) {     tbl <- tabulate(row + 1L, nbins = 2)     which.max(tbl) - 1L   }) }  z_mode_b <- recover_field(result_b, burnin) z_mode_g <- recover_field(result_g, burnin) z_mode_p <- recover_field(result_p, burnin)  field_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(rep(1:8, 8), 4),   y = rep(rep(8:1, each = 8), 4),   value = c(z_true, z_mode_b, z_mode_g, z_mode_p),   panel = rep(c("True field", "Bernoulli", "Gaussian", "Poisson"),               each = n) ) field_df$panel <- factor(field_df$panel,                          levels = c("True field", "Bernoulli",                                     "Gaussian", "Poisson"))  ggplot(field_df, aes(x, y, fill = factor(value))) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "#440154", "1" = "#fde725")) +   coord_equal() +   facet_wrap(~panel, nrow = 1) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(fill = "z")

title: Emission Models
text: Bernoulli c(, b): Use c(1, 1) (uniform) default. Increase b shrink pkp_k toward 0.5 expect weak signal. Gaussian c(mu_0, sigma2_0, alpha_0, beta_0): Set mu_0 near data mean, sigma2_0 large (e.g., 10000) vague location prior, alpha_0 = 2, beta_0 near expected variance weakly informative scale prior. Poisson c(alpha_0, beta_0): prior mean alpha_0 / beta_0. Use c(1, 0.1) diffuse prior mean 10, match expected count range.

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: NaturalUndirectedGraph foundational graph object mdgm. stores undirected graph compressed sparse row (CSR) format provides methods querying structure sampling spanning trees. Three constructor functions create graphs different representations.

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: Edge lists matrices (data frames) two three columns. first two columns 1-indexed vertex pairs. undirected edge must appear twice (per direction). optional third column gives edge weights.
code: # Triangle: 1 -- 2 -- 3 -- 1 edges <- rbind(   c(1, 2), c(2, 1),   c(2, 3), c(3, 2),   c(1, 3), c(3, 1) ) g <- nug_from_edge_list(3, edges) g$nvertices() #> [1] 3 g$nedges() #> [1] 3

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: symmetric matrix nonzero entries indicate edges. entry values used edge weights.
code: # 3x3 grid (rook adjacency) n <- 9 A <- matrix(0, n, n) for (i in 1:n) {   row_i <- (i - 1) %/% 3 + 1   col_i <- (i - 1) %% 3 + 1   for (j in 1:n) {     row_j <- (j - 1) %/% 3 + 1     col_j <- (j - 1) %% 3 + 1     if (abs(row_i - row_j) + abs(col_i - col_j) == 1) {       A[i, j] <- 1     }   } } g <- nug_from_adj_mat(A, seed = 123L) g$nvertices() #> [1] 9 g$nedges() #> [1] 12

title: Working with Undirected Graphs
text: Spanning trees sampled uniformly (edge weights) using Wilson’s algorithm, Aldous-Broder, fast-forward hybrid method. Available methods: "wilson" — Wilson’s algorithm using loop-erased random walks. Efficient graphs. "aldous_broder" — Aldous-Broder algorithm using simple random walk. Simpler slower large graphs. "fast_forward" — Hybrid method switches random walk loop-erased walk.
code: # Sample with Wilson's algorithm (default) tree <- g$sample_spanning_tree("wilson")

title: Model Specification
text: Classical spatial models discrete data typically use Markov random field (MRF), specifies joint distribution undirected graph. However, MRF likelihood involves intractable normalizing constant (partition function), making exact Bayesian inference computationally burdensome. Mixture Directed Graphical Models (MDGM) alternative takes undirected graph input defines mixture compatible directed acyclic graphs (DAGs). DAG admits tractable factorization, MDGM avoids partition function entirely. joint distribution marginalizes DAG space: p(𝐳∣𝛏)=∑Dp(D)p(𝐳∣D,𝛏)p(\mathbf{z} \mid \boldsymbol{\xi}) = \sum_D p(D)\, p(\mathbf{z} \mid D, \boldsymbol{\xi}). mdgm package supports spatial model types unified interface: use srf_model() either mdgm() mrf() configuration. full details, see Carter Calder (2024).

title: Model Specification
text: models created via srf_model() spatial configuration:
code: # MDGM with spanning tree model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree"))  # MRF with exchange algorithm model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mrf(method = "exchange"))  # Add an emission layer for hierarchical models model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(), emission = "bernoulli") model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mrf(method = "pseudo_likelihood"),                    emission = "gaussian", n_colors = 3L)

title: Model Specification
text: Let G=(V,E)G = (V, E) undirected graph (“natural undirected graph” NUG) encoding potential neighbor relationships. MDGM places prior DAGs DD compatible GG: every directed edge (u,v)(u, v) DD corresponds undirected edge {u,v}\{u, v\} GG. Three DAG constructions supported:

title: Model Specification
text: spanning tree TT GG connected, acyclic subgraph containing vertices. Edges directed child parent. posterior sampling uses Wilson’s algorithm data-dependent edge weights: w(u,v)=exp⁡(ψ⋅𝟏[zu=zv])w(u, v) = \exp\bigl(\psi \cdot \mathbf{1}[z_u = z_v]\bigr) ψ>0\psi > 0 spatial dependence parameter zz color assignment. provides direct (non-MH) posterior sample spanning tree.

title: Model Specification
text: acyclic orientation assigns direction every edge GG directed cycle exists. Equivalently, defined vertex permutation σ\sigma: edge {u,v}\{u, v\} directed (u,v)(u, v) σ(u)>σ(v)\sigma(u) > \sigma(v). MCMC proposes new permutations accepts via Metropolis-Hastings step based exact DAG log-likelihood ratio.

title: Model Specification
text: rooted DAG constructed GG choosing root vertex orienting edges via breadth-first depth-first traversal. MCMC updates root via random walk proposals GG.

title: Model Specification
text: Markov random field (MRF) defines Potts/Ising model undirected graph GG. joint distribution : p(𝐳∣ψ)=1C(ψ)exp⁡(ψ∑{u,v}∈E𝟏[zu=zv])p(\mathbf{z} \mid \psi) = \frac{1}{C(\psi)} \exp\Bigl(\psi \sum_{\{u,v\} \E} \mathbf{1}[z_u = z_v]\Bigr) C(ψ)C(\psi) intractable normalizing constant (partition function). Unlike MDGM, graph structure fixed — DAG sampling performed.

title: Model Specification
text: exchange algorithm (Murray, Ghahramani, MacKay, 2006) cancels partition function introducing auxiliary variable. MCMC iteration: Propose ψ*∼q(⋅∣ψ)\psi^* \sim q(\cdot \mid \psi) (normal random walk) Sample auxiliary field 𝐳*\mathbf{z}^* MRF ψ*\psi^* via Gibbs sweeps Accept probability: min⁡(1,exp⁡[S(𝐳)(ψ*−ψ)+S(𝐳*)(ψ−ψ*)]⋅p(ψ*)p(ψ))\min\Bigl(1,\; \exp\bigl[S(\mathbf{z})(\psi^* - \psi) + S(\mathbf{z}^*)(\psi - \psi^*)\bigr] \cdot \frac{p(\psi^*)}{p(\psi)}\Bigr) S(𝐳)=∑{u,v}∈E𝟏[zu=zv]S(\mathbf{z}) = \sum_{\{u,v\} \E} \mathbf{1}[z_u = z_v] sufficient statistic. n_aux_sweeps parameter controls number Gibbs sweeps used approximately sample 𝐳*\mathbf{z}^* (default: 200). method exact (quality auxiliary sample) expensive per iteration.

title: Model Specification
text: pseudo-likelihood replaces joint likelihood product full conditionals: PL(ψ)=∏∈Vp(zi∣z−,ψ)=∏∈Vexp⁡(ψ⋅ni,zi)∑kexp⁡(ψ⋅nik)\text{PL}(\psi) = \prod_{\V} p(z_i \mid z_{-}, \psi) = \prod_{\V} \frac{\exp(\psi \cdot n_{,z_i})}{\sum_k \exp(\psi \cdot n_{ik})} nikn_{ik} number neighbors vertex ii color kk. tractable approximation uses MH random walk MDGM. fast may underestimate ψ\psi.

title: Model Specification
text: Given DAG DD (MDGM) undirected graph GG (MRF), conditional distribution color ziz_i given neighbors : MDGM (parents DAG): p(zi=k∣zpa(),ψ)∝exp⁡(ψ∑j∈pa()𝟏[zj=k]+αk)p(z_i = k \mid z_{\text{pa}()}, \psi) \propto   \exp\Bigl(\psi \sum_{j \\text{pa}()} \mathbf{1}[z_j = k] + \alpha_k\Bigr) MRF (neighbors undirected graph): p(zi=k∣z−,ψ)∝exp⁡(ψ∑j∈nbr()𝟏[zj=k])p(z_i = k \mid z_{-}, \psi) \propto   \exp\Bigl(\psi \sum_{j \\text{nbr}()} \mathbf{1}[z_j = k]\Bigr) αk\alpha_k marginal log-probabilities (currently fixed 0 uniform marginal MDGM).

code: model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree")) model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mrf(method = "exchange"))

title: Model Specification
text: hierarchical model, zz latent field observations yiy_i generated emission distribution: yij∣zi,θ∼f(yij∣θzi)y_{ij} \mid z_i, \theta \sim f(y_{ij} \mid \theta_{z_i}) Currently supported emission families: Bernoulli: yij∣zi=k∼Bernoulli(pk)y_{ij} \mid z_i = k \sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_k), identifiability constraint p0<p1<⋯p_0 < p_1 < \cdots enforced via truncated Beta posterior sampling. Gaussian: yij∣zi=k∼𝒩(μk,σk2)y_{ij} \mid z_i = k \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_k, \sigma_k^2), identifiability constraint μ0<μ1<⋯\mu_0 < \mu_1 < \cdots. Independent Normal Inverse-Gamma conjugate updates μk\mu_k σk2\sigma_k^2. Poisson: yij∣zi=k∼Poisson(λk)y_{ij} \mid z_i = k \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda_k), identifiability constraint λ0<λ1<⋯\lambda_0 < \lambda_1 < \cdots. Conjugate truncated Gamma updates. MCMC additionally updates zz (Gibbs scan vertices) emission parameters (conjugate updates). See Emission Models vignette worked examples.
code: model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(), emission = "bernoulli") model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mrf(method = "pseudo_likelihood"),                    emission = "gaussian")

title: Model Specification
text: spatial dependence parameter ψ>0\psi > 0 half-Cauchy prior: p(ψ)=2π(1+ψ2),ψ>0p(\psi) = \frac{2}{\pi(1 + \psi^2)}, \quad \psi > 0 Updates use Metropolis-Hastings random walk normal proposal (MDGM MRF pseudo-likelihood) exchange algorithm (MRF exchange).

title: Model Specification
text: Bernoulli: pkp_k Beta(,b)\text{Beta}(, b) prior. emission_prior_params = c(, b). Gaussian: μk∼𝒩(μ0,σ02)\mu_k \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_0, \sigma^2_0) σk2∼InverseGamma(α0,β0)\sigma_k^2 \sim \text{InverseGamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0), independently. emission_prior_params = c(mu_0, sigma2_0, alpha_0, beta_0). Poisson: λk\lambda_k Gamma(α0,β0)\text{Gamma}(\alpha_0, \beta_0) prior (rate parameterization). emission_prior_params = c(alpha_0, beta_0). conjugate posteriors use truncated sampling enforce parameter ordering identifiability.

title: Model Specification
text: iteration MCMC sampler performs: Update graph (MDGM ) — Sample new spanning tree (direct posterior sample via Wilson’s) propose new acyclic orientation/root (MH step). MRF, step -op. Update zz (hierarchical ) — Gibbs scan vertices random order. full conditional combines spatial prior emission likelihood. Update ψ\psi — Metropolis-Hastings normal random walk proposal (default), exchange algorithm MRF method = "exchange". Update θ\theta (hierarchical ) — Conjugate posterior sampling identifiability constraints.

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: mdgm package implements Bayesian inference discrete spatial random fields. supports two spatial model families: Mixture Directed Graphical Models (MDGM) — defines mixture directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) compatible undirected graph. DAG admits tractable likelihood, avoiding partition function entirely. Markov Random Field (MRF) — classical Potts/Ising model graph. Inference dependence parameter uses either exchange algorithm (exact) pseudo-likelihood (approximate). can combined emission distributions (Bernoulli, Gaussian, Poisson) hierarchical models spatial field latent. MDGM approach described : Carter, J. B. Calder, C. . (2024). Mixture Directed Graphical Models Discrete Spatial Random Fields. arXiv:2406.15700

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: Create 4x4 grid graph rook adjacency using nug_from_grid(): Graphs can also constructed adjacency matrix, edge list, adjacency list — see Working Undirected Graphs vignette.

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: igraph package (suggested, required) provides quick way visualize neighborhood structure:
code: library(igraph) #>  #> Attaching package: 'igraph' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     decompose, spectrum #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     union  # Build igraph object from the NUG edge structure n <- nug$nvertices() el <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:n, function(v) {   nbrs <- nug$neighbors(v)   nbrs <- nbrs[nbrs > v]   if (length(nbrs) == 0) return(NULL)   cbind(v, nbrs) })) g <- graph_from_edgelist(el, directed = FALSE)  # Grid layout matching the spatial positions coords <- cbind((seq_len(n) - 1) %% 4 + 1, 4 - (seq_len(n) - 1) %/% 4)  plot(g, layout = coords, vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = 1:n,      vertex.label.color = "white", vertex.color = "steelblue",      edge.color = "grey40", main = "4x4 Grid Graph")

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: standalone model, spatial field zz observed directly — emission distribution needed. fit spanning-tree MDGM deterministic checkerboard pattern 4x4 grid: can color graph vertices field values:  visualize raster:
code: z <- c(0L, 0L, 0L, 1L,        0L, 0L, 1L, 1L,        1L, 1L, 1L, 0L,        1L, 1L, 0L, 0L)  model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree"))  result <- mcmc(model, z_init = z, psi_init = 0.5,                n_iter = 2000L, psi_tune = 1.0, seed = 42L) result$summary() #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 2000 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.478 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.8446 (sd: 0.5612) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 1.0013, ESS: 272 plot(g, layout = coords, vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = 1:n,      vertex.color = c("#440154", "#fde725")[z + 1],      vertex.label.color = "white", edge.color = "grey40",      main = "Spatial Field on Grid") grid_df <- data.frame(   x = rep(1:4, times = 4),   y = rep(4:1, each = 4),   z = factor(z) )  ggplot(grid_df, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_raster() +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "#440154", "1" = "#fde725")) +   coord_equal() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(title = "4x4 Spatial Field", fill = "z")

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: Trace plot spatial dependence parameter ψ\psi:  Acceptance rates:
code: psi_df <- data.frame(iteration = seq_along(result$psi()), psi = result$psi())  ggplot(psi_df, aes(iteration, psi)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +   theme_minimal() +   labs(x = "Iteration", y = expression(psi), title = "Psi trace plot") result$acceptance_rates() #>       psi     graph  #> 0.4777389 0.0000000

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: edge_inclusion_probs() method counts often undirected edge appears posterior spanning-tree samples. can use proportions scale edge widths:  Edges -colored vertices appear frequently posterior spanning trees, reflecting spatial dependence captured ψ\psi.
code: eip <- result$edge_inclusion_probs(nug, burnin = 200L) head(eip[order(-eip$prob), ]) #>    vertex1 vertex2      prob #> 7        4       8 0.8116667 #> 21      12      16 0.8111111 #> 24      15      16 0.7794444 #> 3        2       3 0.7700000 #> 1        1       2 0.7238889 #> 2        1       5 0.7177778  # Match edge inclusion probs to igraph edge ordering edge_probs <- numeric(ecount(g)) for (i in seq_len(nrow(eip))) {   eid <- get.edge.ids(g, c(eip$vertex1[i], eip$vertex2[i]))   edge_probs[eid] <- eip$prob[i] } #> Warning: `get.edge.ids()` was deprecated in igraph 2.1.0. #> ℹ Please use `get_edge_ids()` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.  plot(g, layout = coords, vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = 1:n,      vertex.color = c("#440154", "#fde725")[z + 1],      vertex.label.color = "white",      edge.width = edge_probs * 10,      edge.color = "grey30",      main = "Edge inclusion probabilities")

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: hierarchical model, zz latent field observations generated emission distribution. use Bernoulli emission:
code: model_h <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree"),                      emission = "bernoulli")  # Simulate 5 Bernoulli observations per vertex (multiple needed for identifiability) set.seed(1) p_true <- c(0.2, 0.8) y <- lapply(seq_len(n), function(i) rbinom(5, 1, p_true[z[i] + 1]))  result_h <- mcmc(model_h, y = y, z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                  psi_init = 0.5, theta_init = c(0.3, 0.7),                  n_iter = 500L, seed = 42L) result_h$summary() #> MDGM MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 500 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.896 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.6269 (sd: 0.5127) #>   Emission type: bernoulli #>   p_1 posterior mean: 0.2284 (sd: 0.0792) #>   p_2 posterior mean: 0.8089 (sd: 0.0675) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 1.0124, ESS: 6 #>     p_1 — R-hat: 1.0025, ESS: 63 #>     p_2 — R-hat: 0.9989, ESS: 207

title: Getting Started with mdgm
text: package also supports classical MRF (Potts/Ising) models graph. mrf() configuration helper specifies inference method dependence parameter ψ\psi: "exchange" — exchange algorithm, cancels intractable partition function sampling auxiliary field (exact) "pseudo_likelihood" — replaces joint likelihood product full conditionals (fast, approximate) exchange algorithm expensive per iteration provides exact inference: MRF models can also combined emission distributions hierarchical inference, just like MDGM models: Note MRF results DAG samples edge inclusion probabilities, since graph structure fixed.
code: # MRF with pseudo-likelihood inference model_mrf <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mrf(method = "pseudo_likelihood"))  result_mrf <- mcmc(model_mrf, z_init = z, psi_init = 0.5,                    n_iter = 2000L, psi_tune = 0.5, seed = 42L) result_mrf$summary() #> MRF MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 2000 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.681 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.8784 (sd: 0.4959) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 1.0057, ESS: 158 model_ex <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mrf(method = "exchange",                                          n_aux_sweeps = 100L))  result_ex <- mcmc(model_ex, z_init = z, psi_init = 0.5,                   n_iter = 500L, psi_tune = 0.5, seed = 42L) result_ex$summary() #> MRF MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 500 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.485 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.4567 (sd: 0.2781) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 1.0084, ESS: 56 model_mrf_h <- srf_model(nug,                          spatial = mrf(method = "pseudo_likelihood"),                          emission = "bernoulli")  result_mrf_h <- mcmc(model_mrf_h, y = y,                      z_init = sample(0:1, n, replace = TRUE),                      psi_init = 0.5, theta_init = c(0.3, 0.7),                      n_iter = 500L, seed = 42L) result_mrf_h$summary() #> MRF MCMC Results #>   Vertices: 16, Colors: 2 #>   Iterations: 500 (burnin: 0) #>   Psi acceptance rate: 0.866 #>   Psi posterior mean: 0.5128 (sd: 0.4028) #>   Emission type: bernoulli #>   p_1 posterior mean: 0.2254 (sd: 0.0791) #>   p_2 posterior mean: 0.8159 (sd: 0.0668) #>   Diagnostics: #>     psi — R-hat: 1.2701, ESS: 8 #>     p_1 — R-hat: 1.0071, ESS: 227 #>     p_2 — R-hat: 1.0031, ESS: 278

title: Mixture of Directed Graphical Models
text: Full documentation: https://jbcart.github.io/mdgm/ mdgm provides Bayesian inference discrete spatial random fields. supports two spatial model families undirected graphs: Mixture Directed Graphical Models (MDGM) — avoids intractable MRF partition function defining mixture compatible DAGs. Markov Random Field (MRF) — classical Potts/Ising model inference via exchange algorithm (exact) pseudo-likelihood (approximate). can combined emission distributions (Bernoulli, Gaussian, Poisson) hierarchical models spatial field latent. See Carter Calder (2024) full methodological details.

code: mcmc(   model,   y = NULL,   z_init,   psi_init,   theta_init = numeric(0),   n_iter = 1000L,   psi_tune = 0.1,   emission_prior_params = NULL,   store_z = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   nug = NULL )

title: Run MCMC inference for a spatial random field model — mcmc
text: model SrfModel object created srf_model(). y Observation data. hierarchical models, list numeric vectors y[[]] contains observations vertex . Bernoulli, values 0 1; Poisson, non-negative integers; Gaussian, real values. Vertices observations numeric(0). standalone models, pass NULL (default). z_init Initial color assignment, integer vector length n values 0, ..., n_colors - 1. psi_init Initial value dependence parameter (positive). theta_init Initial emission parameters. Bernoulli, numeric vector length n_colors (e.g., c(0.2, 0.8)). Gaussian, vector length 2 * n_colors means variances (e.g., c(mu_1, mu_2, sigma2_1, sigma2_2)). Poisson, vector length n_colors rate parameters. Ignored standalone models. n_iter Number MCMC iterations (default 1000). psi_tune Standard deviation normal MH proposal psi (default 0.1). emission_prior_params Prior hyperparameters emission parameters. Bernoulli: c(, b) Beta(, b) prior. Gaussian: c(mu_0, sigma2_0, alpha_0, beta_0) mu_k ~ N(mu_0, sigma2_0) sigma_k^2 ~ InvGamma(alpha_0, beta_0) independently. Poisson: c(alpha_0, beta_0) Gamma(alpha_0, beta_0) prior. Default: c(1, 1) Bernoulli/Poisson, c(0, 10000, 0.01, 0.01) Gaussian (non-informative). store_z Logical; TRUE, store full latent field matrix (n x n_iter). Default FALSE conserve memory — large grids (e.g. 1000x1000 10,000 iterations), z matrix alone requires ~40 GB. FALSE, per-iteration summary statistics (allocation counts, sufficient statistics, MAP configuration) still computed. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. nug Optional NaturalUndirectedGraph object. provided, stored result use edge_inclusion_probs() plot().

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a small grid graph A <- matrix(0, 4, 4) A[1, 2] <- A[2, 1] <- A[2, 3] <- A[3, 2] <- 1 A[3, 4] <- A[4, 3] <- 1 nug <- nug_from_adj_mat(A, seed = 42L)  # Standalone model model <- srf_model(nug, spatial = mdgm(dag_type = "spanning_tree")) result <- mcmc(model, z_init = c(0L, 0L, 1L, 1L),                psi_init = 0.5, n_iter = 100L) result$summary() } # }
